outline.text: 2.3.2. Enable access to Palo Alto Networks resources
If the Password Protection (for downloaded files) setting under Settings → Configuration → General → Server Settings is enabled, enter the password 'suspicious' to download the file
Settings → Configurations → Data Broker → Engines → Create New Engine
2.5.6.1. Forward logs and data from Cortex AgentiX to external services
3.2.1.2. Add a new data source or instance
Settings → Configurations → Data Collection → Integration Permissions
Settings → Configurations → Marketplace) or at Cortex Developer Docs Marketplace
3.2.2.6. Content changes when upgrading Cortex AgentiX versions
3.2.3.4. Fetch issues from an integration instance
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Issues → Classification & Mapping
3.2.3.4.2. Classify events using a classifier for issue types
Settings → Configurations → Integrations → Credentials → New Credential
3.2.8. Creating correlation rules to monitor data ingestion health
3.3.2.5. Expand agent capabilities with MCP integrations
3.4.1.4. Create custom Cortex MCP server tools

If the content repository option is disabled for the production or development tenant (under Settings → Configurations → General → Remote Repository Settings, toggle the Content repository slider to off), the tenant becomes standalone and does not push or pull content

If you are changing your remote repository settings, back up existing content to your local computer by navigating to Settings → Configurations → General → Server Settings → Custom Content and click Export all custom content
3.5.2.1. Set up a built-in remote repository
Settings → Configurations → General → Remote Repository Settings and toggle the Content repository slider to enable the remote repository
3.5.2.2. Set up a Private Remote Repository
Settings → Configurations → General → Remote Repository Settings
3.6.1. Users and roles in Cortex AgentiX
3.6.2.1. Manage roles in the Cortex AgentiX tenant
You can manage role permissions in Cortex AgentiX under Settings → Configurations → Access Management → Roles
Users can primarily access Query Library from Investigation & Response → Search → XQL Search, and select the Query Library tab
Users can also view and edit Lists (under Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Lists) provided they have Cases & Issues permissions
Controls Playground Investigation & Response → Automation → Playground, which is a testing environment for commands and scripts that is not connected to a live (active) investigation
Controls access to the Automation Exclusion Center (Settings → Configurations → Automation → Automation Exclusion Center), which prevents a command or script from a remediation action
Located under Threat Management → Threat Intelligence → Indicators, these permissions govern how your organization interacts with indicators (IPs, URLs, Domains, Hashes) and intelligence feeds
Configurations: Manage rules through Settings → Configuration → General → Notifications: Includes main notification rules and user notifications
External forwarding: Configure through Settings → Configuration → Integrations → External Applications
Set permissions for Users, Roles, User Groups, and Authentication Settings under Access Management (Settings → Configurations → Access Management
Data Sources is managed under Settings → Configurations → Data Collection → Data Sources & Integrations
Controls access to dataset configuration, data transformation rules, and data lifecycle management in Cortex AgentiX through Settings → Configurations → Data Management
Controls access to the configuration page for external threat intelligence API keys (Virus Total) on Settings → Configurations → Integrations → Threat Intelligence
Users can access Credentials on the Credentials page by going to Settings → Configurations → Integrations → Credentials
Controls access to the following tabs from Cases (Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Cases

When managing Indicators (located under Threat Management → Threat Intelligence → Indicators), users who lack View/Edit permissions for the Exclusion List will find that exclusion-related features (such as the Exclusion reason field and Do not add to exclusion list checkbox) are automatically hidden by the system
Controls access to the indicator exclusion list configuration under Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Indicators → Exclusion List
Controls access to custom fields and indicator types within Object Setup Settings → Configurations → Object Setup
Controls access within Object Setup (Settings → Configurations → Object Setup) to the following
Controls access to case mirroring profiles configuration in Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Issues → Sync Profiles

Controls access to the AI Prompts Library (Investigation & Response → Automation → AI Prompts), where users create and manage reusable prompt templates (including system instructions and few-shot examples) used to guide the LLM's behavior
By default, Enable Scope Based Access Control is disabled in Settings → Configurations → General → Server Settings, and granular scoping is not enforced
3.6.4.1. Manage users in the Cortex AgentiX tenant
Configure tenant-level settings: An administrator must first establish the sharing framework under Settings → Configurations → Access Management → Objects
3.6.5.3. Manage access to playbooks and scripts
3.6.6.1. Authenticate users through the Customer Support Portal
3.6.6.3. Set up Okta as the Identity Provider Using SAML 2.0
3.6.6.4. Set up Microsoft Entra ID as the Identity Provider Using SAML 2.0
3.7.3.1.1.1. Install Docker distribution for Red Hat on an engine server
You can manage your engines and load-balancing groups by going to Settings → Configurations → Engines
3.7.7.1. Configure the engine to use a web proxy
3.7.7.2. Configure the engine to call the server without using a proxy
3.7.7.2.1. Use NGINX as a reverse proxy
3.7.7.3. Configure an engine to use custom certificates
3.7.8. Use an engine in an integration
3.7.9. Run a script using an engine
When troubleshooting engines, access the logs from Settings → Configurations → Engines and select the engine from which you want to download the logs
You can see all domains under Configurations → Object Setup → Cases → Domains
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Cases → Domains
Settings → Configurations → Access Management → User Groups
To enable access to the domain for an API key, got to Settings → Configurations → Integrations → API Keys
3.8.2.5. Create custom case statuses and resolution reasons
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Issues → Fields → New Field
3.8.3.1.2.1. Create a grid field for an issue
Settings → Configurations → General → Server Settings → Issues and Enable Timer Field
3.8.3.1.2.3.2. Configure a playbook to run timers
3.8.3.1.2.3.3. Automate changes to issue fields using timer scripts
3.8.3.1.2.3.4. Use issue timer field commands manually in the CLI
3.8.3.2.2. Add a custom widget to an issue layout

Investigation & Response → Automation → Scripts and upload the following script: Expand script commonfields: id: ee3b9604-324b-4ab5-8164-15ddf6e428ab version: 49 name: IndicatorWidgetBar script: |- # Constants HIGH = 3 SUSPICIOUS = 2 LOW = 1 NONE = 0 indicators = [] scores = {HIGH: 0, SUSPICIOUS: 0, LOW: 0, NONE: 0} incident_id = demisto
Investigation & Response → Automation → Scripts and upload the following script
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Issues → Layouts
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Issues → Layout Rules → New Rule
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Cases → Fields → New Field
3.8.4.1.2.1. Create a grid field for a case
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Cases and open the Fields tab
3.8.4.1.4. Update case timer and SLA fields
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Cases → Layout Rules → New Rule
You can find a sync profile ID under Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Issues → Sync Profiles
After you configure your correlation rules, you can manage them in Threat Management → Detection Rules → Correlations, and view and analyze the generated issues in Cases and the Issues Table
If you are assigned a role that enables Investigation → Rules privileges, you can manage all user-defined Correlation Rules from Threat Management → Detection Rules → Correlations
You can create a new correlation rule from either the Threat Management → Detection Rules → Correlation Rules page or when building a query in XQL Search
3.9.4.6.1. Task 1. Choose from existing playbooks or create your own
Open the Investigation & Response → Automation → Playbooks page to find an existing playbook, customize it, or create a playbook
3.9.4.6.3. Task 3. Add objects from the Task Library
3.9.4.6.3.4. Add manual tasks and blank tasks
3.9.4.6.3.6. Configure script error handling in a playbook
3.9.4.6.5. Task 5. Test and debug the playbook
Investigation & Response → Automation → Scripts → New Script
3.9.4.7.2.1. Filter considerations, categories, and built-in filters
3.9.4.7.2.2. Transformer considerations, categories, and built-in transformers
3.9.4.7.5. Update issue fields with playbook tasks
On the AI Prompts page (Investigation → Response → Automation → AI Prompts), you can view, edit, and manually create prompts
Investigation & Response → Automation → AI Prompts and in the Prompts Library search for the prompt you want to use
Investigation & Response → Automation → AI Prompts and click + New Prompt
Investigation & Response → Automation → Scripts and in the Scripts Library search for the script you want to use
3.9.8.4. Use the Automation Engineer agent to accelerate script development and deployment
3.9.8.5. Change the Docker image in an integration or script
3.9.9.4. Add context data to an issue
3.9.9.5. Add context data to a case
3.9.9.6. Delete context data from a case
3.9.9.7. Use context data in a playbook
3.10.1.2.2. Download JSON file of lookup dataset
Open the Settings → Configurations → Data Management → Dataset Management page
3.10.1.2.3. Set time to live for lookup datasets
3.10.1.3. Monitor datasets and dataset views activity
Under Settings → Configurations → Data Management → Event Forwarding, activate one or more of the following: Enable GB Event Forwarding
Under Settings → Configurations → Data Management → Event Forwarding, activate one or more of the following
Under Settings → Configurations → General → Notifications, click + Add Forwarding Configuration
3.11.4.1. Create a custom widget using a script
3.11.4.3.1. Configure filters and inputs for custom XQL widgets
You can manage all starring configurations under Case & Issues → Case Configuration → Starred Issues
Configurations → General → Server Settings → Case display modes
Configurations → General → Server Settings → AI Configuration and enable the following settings
4.3.5.3.1. Resolution reasons for cases and issues
To create a case manually, you must have View/Edit permission for Cases and Issues selected under Settings → Configurations → Access Management → Roles → Components → Cases & Issues
4.4.4. Link or unlink issues from a case
4.4.5. Run an automation on an issue
4.4.6. Use the War Room in an investigation
4.4.7. Use the Work Plan in an investigation
4.4.9.3. Export issue details to a file
4.4.9.6. Run indicator extraction in the CLI
4.4.9.8. Use issue timer field commands manually in the CLI
4.5.2. Investigate a file and process hash
5.1. Get started with Agentic Assistant chat
5.3. Chat with an Agentic Assistant agent
5.4. Chat with the Agentic Assistant from Slack
5.5. Create and run XQL queries with Agentic Assistant chat
5.6. Use natural language to query and visualize your data
6.1. Get started with Threat Intel Management
6.1.3. Roles and responsibilities in Threat Intel Management
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Indicators → Types
Settings → Configurations → Object Setup → Indicators → Fields → New Field
6.2.3.1. Set the indicator extraction mode for a playbook task
6.2.3.2. Disable indicator extraction for scripts or integrations
Indicators should be present in the Threat Intelligence database (Threat Management → Threat Intelligence → Indicators) before creating detection rules
You submit XQL queries to Cortex AgentiX using the Investigation & Response → Search → Query Builder user interface
Investigation & Response → Search → Query Builder → XQL → Query Library
7.1.7. How to filter for empty values in the results table
7.2.3.1. Get started with Query Builder templates
7.2.3.2. Considerations for using Query Builder templates
7.2.3.3. Create a query from a template
7.2.4.1. Edit and run queries in Query Center
Investigation & Response → Search → Query Center → Query History
From the Query Builder Select Investigation & Response → Search → Query Builder → XQL
Investigation & Response → Search → Query Builder → XQL
7.3. Cortex XQL syntax, parameters, and examples
9.2. Fair Usage policy for Cortex AgentiX
